source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Scientists have discovered two "super-Earths" of unknown masses, A and B, orbiting the same star in uniform circular motion. Their orbital periods are known to be $T_A = 1$ year and $T_B = 8$ years, respectively.

1. Calculate the ratio of the angular velocities $\omega_A$ : $\omega_B$ of two "super-Earths".
2. Calculate the ratio of the centripetal accelerations of two "super-Earths" $a_A$:$a_B$.

image_dependency: Question is fully stated in text and asks for ratio calculations, not visual attributes or spatial interpretation
multi_step: The problem has two sub-questions, not a single direct lookup
Part 1 requires applying angular velocity-period relation (omega = 2pi/T)
Part 2 requires additional physics reasoning beyond Part 1, combining circular-motion acceleration with orbital-period/orbit-size relations for bodies orbiting the same star
The answer depends on chaining at least two concepts rather than extracting a value directly from the prompt
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and the expected outputs are explicit numeric ratios
Both requested outputs are concrete and can be checked against standard formulas
registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present in the registry record
Low ambiguity in final target: two ratio results corresponding to the two numbered items

decision_reasons: Question and answer are both present, and the registry passed with no recorded asset issues.
The problem is text-complete and has very low image dependency, so the empty image manifest does not block core semantic recovery.
High multi-step and verifiability scores indicate the sample is useful and can be reliably checked.
Risk flags are limited to answer-format variation rather than missing core content.
